node.title: Google | Onsite | Merge K Sorted Iterators

topic.title: Google | Onsite | Merge K Sorted Iterators

post.content:
need to use peeking iterator for each single list and then use PriorityQueue to maintain sorted list.
Ideally combination of peeking iterator and mege k sorted list question

devil in this case is in details or your implementation
Put iterators in a min heap and advance them, removing them when they equals the ends of their vecs (I\'d use pairs in the heap).

I believe the expectation here is to solve the problem in O(Nlogk)  or at least O(kN) time complexity where k is the number of iterators & N is the total elements in the final sorted list. The Priority  Queue solution yields O(NlogN) time complexity because it does make use of the fact that iterators are already sorted.

This problem is exactly https://leetcode.com/problems/merge-k-sorted-lists with slight variation.
it is sorted then merge also has 0(nlogk) complexity.

Here's my attempt in Python3,

```python

import heapq
import random
import itertools

def get_data():
    # a simple generator to generate random integers in a sorted fashion (strictly increasing order)
    frac = random.random()
    j = 0
    while True:
        j += 1
        if random.random() < frac:
            continue
        yield j

print("sample data", list(itertools.islice(get_data(), 10)))

# it's lazy :)
merged = heapq.merge(get_data(), get_data())
print("sorted and merged data", list(itertools.islice(merged, 10)))

s1 = (("stream_1", j) for j in get_data()) # stream 1
s2 = (("stream_2", j) for j in get_data()) # stream 2


# NB that we have consumed some elements of the generator here
print("sample data stream_1", list(itertools.islice(s1, 10)))
print("sample data stream_2", list(itertools.islice(s2, 10)))

print()
print()

# merged the sorted data stream's to get back the sorted data points.
for idx, data in enumerate(heapq.merge(s1, s2, key=lambda x: x[1])):
    print(data, end = "")
    if idx > 100:
        break

```
Do you guys know any C++ solution for this? I was wondering if it is necessary the peek iterator in C++ as well.
Does anyone knows solution for this?
